Understanding the epidemiology of infectious agents causing nosocomial infections is crucial but complex. Diagnostic challenges including low sensitivity (limited blood volume) and specificity (contamination risk) are inherent to the neonatal population. Additionally, availability of microbiological diagnostics and resources can significantly affect detection rates.

In your data, `r dR$own$numberOfInfectiousAgents` infectious agents were detected in `r dR$own$numberOfInfectionsWithAgent` infections out of `r dR$own$overallNumberOfInfections` total infections (including sepsis/BSI, pneumonia, necrotising enterocolitis, and surgical site infections), representing an infectious agent detection rate of `r dR$own$infectiousAgentDetectionRate` per 100 infections. The following tables provide detailed information on pathogen distribution and antimicrobial resistance patterns.
